Title: Per-panel reference (delta% denominator is instance state; NO global star)
Notes: statsRefDs (module-global today; moves to instance state at 4e) — NO global ★. statsReferenceDs() defaults to the first SHOWN comparison (samples) so the headline stays (model−samples)/samples; self-heals if the chosen ref is hidden/removed. Δ% generalized: every NON-reference dataset (model included) shows a Δ% row = (subject−ref)/|ref| beneath its row (deltaRowFor + statsRefStatsFor/statsRefLabelFor); the reference row is badged 'ref' (stats-ref-badge; inferred-state-visible per no-magic-only-ui). Reference picker (#statsRefSel: Model + shown comparisons) shows in the Datasets section at ≥2 comparisons (where ambiguous); lone-comparison case stays uncluttered (ref unambiguously = it, badged). Default model+aux number IDENTICAL to pre-4d (−7.3% Fe), just relocated under the model (subject) with aux badged. Covered: delta-row-smoke (model carries Δ vs aux ref + badge), a10-smoke (picker present, defaults aux, switch→model flips badge + d2 Δ vs model). Ephemeral until 4e/phase-6 serialization.

Title: Import unification (grid-detect any dataset; geometry section conditional)
Notes:
Merges model-preflight with aux-import; biggest new surface. SCOPED 2026-06-17 (Explore-mapped the two import surfaces: model=preflight.js/#panelPreflight w/ XYZ+DXYZ+geometry; comparisons=auxtab.js/#panelAux clones, XYZ-only, geometry discarded). DECISION (Arthur): grid classification = AUTO-detect + VISIBLE + OVERRIDE (no-magic-only-ui). Slices: 4f-1 foundation · 4f-2 import UI (conditional geometry/DXYZ section + grid badge/override in dataset panels) · 4f-3 display (generalize Summary Grid-Geometry table beyond model-only lastGeoData) · 4f-4 de-special-case naming/branches. **4f-1 DONE: worker computeGeometry emits per-axis `regularity` (dominant-spacing share of gaps) + geometry-level `isRegularGrid` (all 3 axes blockSize + regularity>=0.5) — ADDITIVE (b1-differential: all 29 cases byte-identical w/ --ignore-keys=isRegularGrid,regularity; existing geometry/stats unchanged). Comparison-dataset complete handler now captures m.geometry (was discarded). dsGridMode(ds) tri-state grid/point/auto (default model='grid' [explicit block-model import], else 'auto'); dsGrid gates: blockSize-on-all-axes AND (mode grid | (auto & isRegularGrid)); point forces null. Model bit-identical (grandfathered via gridMode default). a10-smoke: model still grid, d2(model centroids) auto-detects grid, gridMode='point' override forces non-grid, aux(samples) not a grid. c6/gt-theo/topcut green. ds.gridMode override is in-memory in 4f-1; UI + persistence land in 4f-2. **4f-2 DONE (2026-06-17): Block geometry section in BOTH import surfaces (model preflight #pfGridWrap + comparison panels [data-aux=gridWrap]). Grid/point/auto override = segmented .ds-grid-chip control (model default grid, comparisons auto) + post-analysis detected badge (▦ grid <sizes> / ∴ points) w/ disagreement note — no-magic-only-ui. Shared dsGridSectionHtml(ds)+dsHasXYZ(ds) in core.js; badge refresh via refreshModelGridSection() (displayResults) + refreshAuxGridSection(ds,root) (aux complete) since neither sidebar rebuilds post-analysis. gridMode now a real registry field: model/aux views back currentGridMode/auxGridMode, dsCreate carries it for d2+. PERSIST 3 keys (preflight.gridMode/aux.gridMode/datasets[].gridMode) restored in applyProject/applyAuxRestore/wsRestoreInstance, reset at model+aux clear sites. Mode change never re-runs analysis — only flips dsHasGrid downstream (dsGridModeChanged refreshes tree today; 4f-3/4g/4h hook in later). DXYZ sub-block for comparisons scoped OUT (Arthur). NEW GUARD experiments/4f-gridmode-smoke.js (22 asserts — ADD TO REGRESSION LIST). Worker untouched: b1-differential 29/29 bit-identical. a10/c6/delta-row/4e-persist/rails/tree/dock-persist/gt-theo/swath-clone/stats-clone green. Title: Per-dataset filter from context menu + filtered-size preview
Notes: Arthur 2026-06-16. Tree-ds datasetProvider gets Filter… (✓ when set) → #dsFilterModal editing that dataset's .filter (createExprInput, filter mode). Worker 'count' mode (countAnalysis: makeRowSource + src.stream, one pass, counts total + filter survivors, surfaces filterErrors/calcolErrors) → count-complete {total,kept}. Apply sets ds.filter + re-analyzes (model=startAnalysis+currentFilter sync, cmp=runAuxAnalysis which reads ds.filter; syncs an open config-panel filter textarea). Dataset-generic. INSTANT estimate (Arthur's steer — model from the t-digests, NOT the file head): dsFilterSynthEstimate Monte-Carlos 4000 synthetic rows from each column's t-digest centroids + category counts (cumulative-weight arrays, binary-searched; reuses generateSyntheticRows sampling), runs the real compiled filter (+calcols). Sort-order UNBIASED + accurate for single-column predicates (smoke: Fe>50 → modeled 55% vs exact 53%, vs the biased file HEAD's 0%). CAVEAT surfaced honestly ('modeled — assumes columns vary independently, so multi-column filters may differ'): marginals lose JOINT correlation, so multi-column filters on correlated cols can be off. Head-sniff kept only as the not-yet-analyzed fallback (labeled non-representative). Reliable size = the exact one-pass count. Smoke experiments/filter-modal-smoke.js (modeled≈exact, exact proven against post-Apply rowCount). b1-differential 29/29 bit-identical. NEXT polish if wanted: auto-exact for small files.

Title: Copula-modeled filter estimate (OPTIONAL — multi-column accuracy)
Notes: Optional follow-on to A14 — improves the INSTANT filtered-size estimate for MULTI-COLUMN filters on correlated columns (today dsFilterSynthEstimate samples each column's marginal independently, so joint correlation is lost). Discussed Arthur+Claude 2026-06-16. Literal multivariate t-digest = NOT a thing (no canonical total order / quantile in >=2D). Realistic path = GAUSSIAN COPULA: keep the existing per-column t-digest marginals + accumulate ONE d×d correlation matrix in the SAME analysis streaming pass (Welford sum-of-products, no extra I/O); estimate-time = Cholesky → draw correlated normals → push through each column's t-digest inverse-CDF → run the real filter. COST: memory O(d²) trivial (d=30→~3.7KB vs digests' 10s-100s KB); CPU O(d²)/row added to the analysis hot path — free at d≤~20, ~seconds at d=100 over 1M rows (CAP via top-K cols or a d-threshold). CAPTURES monotone/linear dependence (good for grades); MISSES nonlinear/tail dependence; categoricals don't fit Pearson (one-hot blows up d → keep them marginal). RECOMMENDATION (Claude): do NOT bake into the default analyze pass — it taxes a hot path for ALL users to improve ONE preview on a SUBSET of filters, and the exact one-pass count is already truth-on-tap. Build only as OPT-IN / lazy (compute the matrix only when enabled or d below a cap), or skip entirely and rely on Exact count. Single-column predicates already accurate w/o it. Needs: corr matrix in worker complete msg + analysisFingerprint + serialize/cache plumbing.
